mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) across 50+ observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real GDP and retail sales indicate a broad demand contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or earnings revisions remain positive despite the macro contraction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity utilization approaching maximums, prompting inflation hedging flows that generate positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during late-cycle periods, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0 percent imposes severe financing constraints on highly leveraged firms, triggering margin calls and risk-off positioning that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or wage growth data contradicts the employment trend within 30 days.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which translates into higher corporate revenue expectations and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: HOUST growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 40+ monthly observations, or housing market data shows structural decoupling from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below the 2017 baseline during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks that force central bank tightening and trigger negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO values below 100 fail to predict negative 5-day returns during recovery regimes, or the signal loses predictive power when global commodity prices decline simultaneously.
